I have a list of agent skills I want to distribute for myself or for my friends. I will keep them in some git repo in standard directories structure. There will also be some Claude plugins etc. to deploy so we need generalize a bit.

I need some cli responsible for "deploying of those skills" By deploying I mean copying them to relevant directories in user's home directory with a command npx inception-engine "some directory" We can assume that skill always overwrite it's previous version if that one exists or they will use symlinks on POSIX systems. Do not focus on CLI experience at first. I want to have a configuration model figured out first so no fancy CLI frameworks unless I ask for it.

CLI should be cross platform (macOS, Windows 11, linux in that order) so built with node.js. Assume modern version with TypeScript support. Use Typescript configured the most modern way with erasable syntax only. It should also work with sudo or any kind of privilege escalation.

I want it to work cross agent. It would be good to detect what is installed for given user. We are working with skills so they are everywhere but we might want to add Claude subcommands so that would not transfer to e.g Codex. Assume that if a feature is not supported, then it's not copied. Remeber, only skills for now. We have to support Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI. We want to support Antigravity, OpenCode and Github Copilot.

Cross agent design requires a config map what can be copied for each agent and how.  We probably need a manifest that a directory provides describing what is skill, what is MCP what is agent rules. Rememeber to create abstraction only and work on skills.

This project will not hold any useful skills so let's assume that there are some fake test ones. You can be creative with them - like adding support for Jedi Force powers. We need a dry run mode available.

We need revert operation that removes what manifest declared.

Update @README.md file with a package documentation for it's user. Compatability matrix should be added there.

## Research Report: AI Coding Agents - Skills/Custom Commands Storage Locations

Based on my thorough research, here's the complete information about where each AI coding agent stores skills and custom commands:

---

### **1. Claude Code**

**Storage Directories:**
- **macOS/Linux User Skills:** `~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **macOS/Linux Project Skills:** `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **Windows User Skills:** `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\*\SKILL.md`
- **Windows Project Skills:** `.claude\skills\*\SKILL.md`
- **Alternative Project Paths:** `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md` (also checked)

**File Format:** SKILL.md (Markdown with YAML frontmatter)

**Required Frontmatter Fields:**
```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: Clear description of what this skill does
---
```

**Requirements:**
- Name: max 64 characters, lowercase letters/numbers/hyphens only, no reserved words
- Description: max 1024 characters, non-empty
- Markdown body with instructions (recommended <500 lines)
- Optional: `scripts/`, `references/`, `assets/` subdirectories for supporting files

**Manifest/Configuration:** `~/.claude/settings.json` for global settings; `.claude/settings.json` for project-specific; `.claude/settings.local.json` for personal preferences

**Sources:**
- [Extend Claude with skills - Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills)
- [How to Set Up and Use Google Antigravity | Codecademy](https://www.codecademy.com/article/how-to-set-up-and-use-google-antigravity)

---

### **2. OpenAI Codex**

**Storage Directories:**
- **macOS/Linux Global Instructions:** `~/.codex/AGENTS.md` or `~/.codex/AGENTS.override.md`
- **macOS/Linux User Skills:** `~/.codex/skills/*/SKILL.md` or `~/.codex/skills/.system/` for system skills
- **macOS/Linux Project Skills:** `.codex/skills/*/SKILL.md` (checked from project root up to current directory)
- **Windows:** `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\` equivalents

**File Format:** 
- **Instructions:** AGENTS.md or AGENTS.override.md (plain Markdown, no frontmatter required)
- **Skills:** SKILL.md (Markdown with YAML frontmatter)

**AGENTS.md Format:**
- Standard Markdown with no required structure
- Override precedence: `AGENTS.override.md` supersedes `AGENTS.md`
- Files concatenated from root down; files closer to current directory override earlier guidance
- Size limit: 32 KiB default (configurable via `project_doc_max_bytes`)

**Manifest/Configuration:** `.codex/config.json` for configuration

**Sources:**
- [Custom instructions with AGENTS.md – Codex | OpenAI Developers](https://developers.openai.com/codex/guides/agents-md)
- [Agent Skills – Codex | OpenAI Developers](https://developers.openai.com/codex/skills)

---

### **3. Google Gemini CLI**

**Storage Directories:**
- **macOS/Linux User Skills:** `~/.gemini/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **macOS/Linux Project Skills:** `.gemini/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **macOS/Linux Alternative Project:** `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md` (higher precedence)
- **macOS/Linux User Commands:** `~/.gemini/commands/`
- **macOS/Linux Project Commands:** `.gemini/commands/`
- **Windows:** `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\` equivalents

**File Format:** SKILL.md (Markdown with YAML frontmatter)

**Required Frontmatter Fields:**
```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: Clear description of what this skill does and when to use it
---
```

**Requirements:**
- Name: unique identifier matching directory name
- Description: single-line string; primary triggering mechanism for skill invocation
- Description should include "when to use" context
- Markdown body with instructions
- Optional: `scripts/`, `references/`, `assets/` subdirectories

**Manifest/Configuration:** `~/.gemini/config.json` for user settings; `.gemini/config.json` for project settings

**Sources:**
- [Agent Skills | Gemini CLI](https://geminicli.com/docs/cli/skills/)
- [Creating Agent Skills | Gemini CLI](https://geminicli.com/docs/cli/creating-skills/)

---

### **4. Antigravity**

**Storage Directories:**
- **macOS/Linux Global Skills:** `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **macOS/Linux Project Skills:** `.agent/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **Windows:** `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\antigravity\skills\` equivalents
- **Alternative Global:** `~/.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **Alternative Project:** `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md`

**File Format:** SKILL.md (Markdown with YAML frontmatter - universal format)

**Required Frontmatter Fields:**
```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: A clear description of what this skill does
---
```

**Requirements:**
- Uses universal SKILL.md format (compatible with Claude Code, Gemini CLI, others)
- Name: lowercase, alphanumeric with hyphens
- Description: specific and includes keywords; serves as skill's "advertisement"
- Markdown body with instructions (<500 lines recommended)
- Optional: `scripts/`, `references/`, `assets/` subdirectories
- Optional: `.antigravity/rules/` for global guidelines

**Manifest/Configuration:** `.antigravity/README.md` for project overview; configuration via `.antigravity/` directory structure

**Sources:**
- [AntiGravity Agent Skills Setup Guide: Creating the Perfect Habitat for AI Agents | HonoGear](https://www.honogear.com/en/blog/engineering/antigravity-guide-2026)
- [How to Build Custom Skills in Google Antigravity: 5 Practical Examples | Google Cloud - Community](https://medium.com/google-cloud/tutorial-getting-started-with-antigravity-skills-864041811e0d)

---

### **5. OpenCode**

**Storage Directories:**
- **macOS/Linux Global Commands:** `~/.config/opencode/commands/*.md`
- **macOS/Linux Project Commands:** `.opencode/commands/*.md`
- **macOS/Linux Global Skills:** `~/.config/opencode/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **macOS/Linux Project Skills:** `.opencode/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **Alternative Project Skills:** `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` or `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\opencode\` or `%USERPROFILE%\.config\opencode\` equivalents

**File Format:**
- **Commands:** Markdown files with optional YAML frontmatter
- **Skills:** SKILL.md (Markdown with YAML frontmatter)

**Skills Frontmatter Requirements:**
```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: Skill description
license: MIT (optional)
compatibility: opencode (optional)
metadata: (optional - string-to-string map)
---
```

**Skills Name Validation:**
- 1-64 characters
- Lowercase alphanumeric with single hyphen separators
- Must match directory name
- Regex: `^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$`

**Commands Format:**
- Markdown files in `~/.config/opencode/commands/` or `.opencode/commands/`
- Filename (without extension) becomes command ID
- Optional YAML frontmatter with: `template`, `description`, `agent`, `model`
- Supports named arguments using `$NAME` placeholders
- File inclusion via `@filename` syntax

**Manifest/Configuration:** `~/.config/opencode/config.json` for configuration

**Sources:**
- [Agent Skills | OpenCode](https://opencode.ai/docs/skills/)
- [Commands | OpenCode](https://opencode.ai/docs/commands/)

---

### **6. GitHub Copilot**

**Storage Directories:**
- **macOS/Linux Project Skills:** `.github/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **macOS/Linux Personal Skills:** `~/.copilot/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **macOS/Linux Alternative Project:** `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **macOS/Linux Alternative Personal:** `~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md`
- **Repository Custom Instructions:** `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
- **Path-specific Instructions:** `.github/instructions/*.instructions.md` or `~/.github/instructions/*.instructions.md`
- **Windows:** `%USERPROFILE%\.copilot\` equivalents

**File Format:** SKILL.md (Markdown with YAML frontmatter)

**Required Frontmatter Fields:**
```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: Description of what the skill does and when to use it
license: (optional)
---
```

**Requirements:**
- Name: lowercase, using hyphens for spaces
- Description: 10-1024 characters, determines when agents activate the skill
- Directory names: lowercase with hyphens (e.g., `microsoft-docs`, `azure-deployment`)
- Markdown body with instructions (sections: Purpose/Workflow, Tool References, Step-by-Step Procedures, Examples, Error Handling, Critical Rules)
- Optional: supporting scripts, examples, or other resources

**Manifest/Configuration:** `~/.copilot/config.json` for configuration

**Sources:**
- [Creating agent skills for GitHub Copilot CLI - GitHub Docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/customize-copilot/create-skills)
- [Adding repository custom instructions for GitHub Copilot - GitHub Docs](https://docs.github.com/copilot/customizing-copilot/adding-custom-instructions-for-github-copilot)

---

## Summary Table

| Agent | User Skills Path | Project Skills Path | File Format | Key Config |
|-------|------------------|-------------------|-------------|-----------|
| Claude Code | `~/.claude/skills/` | `.claude/skills/` | SKILL.md | `~/.claude/settings.json` |
| OpenAI Codex | `~/.codex/skills/` | `.codex/skills/` | SKILL.md | `~/.codex/AGENTS.md` |
| Gemini CLI | `~/.gemini/skills/` | `.gemini/skills/` | SKILL.md | `~/.gemini/config.json` |
| Antigravity | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/` | `.agent/skills/` | SKILL.md | `.antigravity/` directory |
| OpenCode | `~/.config/opencode/skills/` | `.opencode/skills/` | SKILL.md | `~/.config/opencode/config.json` |
| GitHub Copilot | `~/.copilot/skills/` | `.github/skills/` | SKILL.md | `.github/copilot-instructions.md` |

All agents follow a similar pattern with SKILL.md files containing YAML frontmatter (name + description required) followed by Markdown instructions, making skills highly portable across different AI coding agents.

---

---
```

- Name: unique identifier matching directory name
- Description: single-line string; primary triggering mechanism for skill invocation
- Description should include "when to use" context
- Markdown body with instructions
- Optional: `scripts/`, `references/`, `assets/` subdirectories

**Manifest/Configuration:** `~/.gemini/config.json` for user settings; `.gemini/config.json` for project settings

|-------|------------------|-------------------|-------------|-----------|
| Claude Code | `~/.claude/skills/` | `.claude/skills/` | SKILL.md | `~/.claude/settings.json` |

Design an implementation plan for "inception-engine" - a cross-platform Node.js CLI tool that deploys AI agent skills from a git repo to user home directories.

## Current Repo State
- Nearly empty: just README.md, LICENSE, .gitignore, .claude/settings.json, .entire/ config
- No package.json, no tsconfig.json, no source code yet

## Requirements
1. **CLI invocation**: `npx inception-engine <directory>` where directory is a repo containing skills
2. **Cross-platform**: macOS, Windows 11, Linux
3. **TypeScript**: Modern config with erasable syntax only (no enums, no namespaces, etc.)
4. **Deployment method**: Symlinks on POSIX, copy on Windows. Always overwrites previous version.
5. **Privilege escalation**: Must work with sudo
6. **Dry-run mode**: Show what would be done without doing it
7. **Revert operation**: Remove what manifest declared
8. **No fancy CLI framework** - keep it simple, just process.argv parsing
9. **Only skills for now** - manifest should describe skill/MCP/agent-rules but only skills are implemented

## Agent Skill Paths (user-level deployment)
All agents use SKILL.md format with YAML frontmatter (name + description).

| Agent | User Skills Path (POSIX) | User Skills Path (Windows) | Supported Features |
|-------|-------------------------|---------------------------|-------------------|
| Claude Code | `~/.claude/skills/<name>/` | `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\<name>\` | skills |
| OpenAI Codex | `~/.codex/skills/<name>/` | `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\skills\<name>\` | skills |
| Gemini CLI | `~/.gemini/skills/<name>/` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\skills\<name>\` | skills |
| Antigravity | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/<name>/` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\antigravity\skills\<name>\` | skills |

| GitHub Copilot | `~/.copilot/skills/<name>/` | `%USERPROFILE%\.copilot\skills\<name>\` | skills |

## Agent Detection
Need to detect which agents are installed. Possible approaches:
- Check if the agent's config directory exists (e.g., ~/.claude/ exists means Claude Code is likely installed)
- Check if the agent binary is in PATH

## Manifest Format
The source repo needs a manifest file (e.g., `inception-engine.json` or `manifest.json`) at the root that describes what's in the repo:

```json
{
  "skills": [
    {
      "name": "jedi-force-powers",
      "path": "skills/jedi-force-powers",
      "agents": ["claude-code", "codex", "gemini-cli", "antigravity", "opencode", "github-copilot"]

## Project Structure Proposal

├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          # CLI entry point
│   ├── config/
│   │   ├── agents.ts     # Agent registry with paths per platform
│   │   └── manifest.ts   # Manifest schema/types

---

# Inception Engine -- Implementation Plan

## 1. Design Decisions (Resolved)

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Manifest file name | `inception.json` | Short, unambiguous, follows `package.json` / `tsconfig.json` convention |
| Symlink strategy | Symlink the entire skill directory (POSIX); recursive copy on Windows | One symlink per skill is simpler, atomic, and easier to revert. A directory symlink preserves future multi-file skills. |
| Agent detection | Config directory existence check first, binary-in-PATH as secondary | Config dirs are created on first run for all listed agents; binary check catches agents installed but never launched. |
| TypeScript execution | `node --experimental-strip-types` for development; compile to JS for the published npm package | Node 25 supports this natively. The `bin` entry in package.json points to the compiled `dist/index.js` so npx works on Node 20+ without flags. |
| Package bin entry | `"bin": { "inception-engine": "./dist/index.js" }` | Standard approach. A `prepublishOnly` script compiles TS to JS. |
| Home directory resolution under sudo | `SUDO_USER` env var on POSIX to get real user's home; `os.homedir()` as fallback | Running `sudo npx inception-engine .` would resolve `~` to `/root` without this. |

## 2. Project Setup

### 2a. `package.json`

```jsonc
{
  "name": "inception-engine",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Deploy AI agent skills from a git repo to user home directories",
  "license": "MIT",
  "author": "Damian Piatkowski",
  "type": "module",
  "bin": {
    "inception-engine": "./dist/index.js"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20.0.0"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc",
    "dev": "node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "npm run build"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "typescript": "^5.8.0"
  }
}
```

Key points:
- `"type": "module"` for ESM throughout.
- Zero runtime dependencies. Only `typescript` as a dev dependency.
- `"files": ["dist"]` keeps the published package lean.
- `engines.node >= 20` because that is the oldest Active LTS that supports the ESM features we need. The compiled JS output targets ES2024 which Node 20 handles.

### 2b. `tsconfig.json`

```jsonc
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2024",
    "module": "NodeNext",
    "moduleResolution": "NodeNext",
    "outDir": "./dist",
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "declaration": true,
    "strict": true,
    "erasableSyntaxOnly": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "verbatimModuleSyntax": true
  },
  "include": ["src"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}
```

`verbatimModuleSyntax` enforces `import type` for type-only imports, which pairs with `erasableSyntaxOnly` to guarantee all type syntax is purely erasable.

## 3. File-by-File Implementation Order

The implementation proceeds in dependency order -- types first, then config, then core logic, then CLI entry point, then test content.

### Phase 1: Types and Configuration

#### File 1: `src/types.ts`

Defines all shared types using only erasable syntax (interfaces, type aliases -- no enums).

```ts
// Agent identifiers as a union type (not an enum)
type AgentId =
  | "claude-code"
  | "codex"
  | "gemini-cli"
  | "antigravity"
  | "opencode"
  | "github-copilot";

// Manifest types
interface SkillEntry {
  name: string;
  path: string;
  agents: AgentId[];
}

interface Manifest {
  skills: SkillEntry[];
  mcpServers: unknown[];   // placeholder for future
  agentRules: unknown[];   // placeholder for future
}

// Agent registry types
interface AgentPaths {
  posix: string;   // template with {home} and {name} placeholders
  windows: string; // template with {home} and {name} placeholders
}

interface AgentConfig {
  id: AgentId;
  displayName: string;
  skills: AgentPaths;
  detectPaths: {
    posix: string;  // directory to check, e.g. "{home}/.claude"
    windows: string;
  };
  detectBinary: string | null; // binary name to look up in PATH
}

// Operation result types
interface DeployAction {
  skill: string;
  agent: AgentId;
  source: string;
  target: string;
  method: "symlink" | "copy";
}

interface RevertAction {
  skill: string;
  agent: AgentId;
  target: string;
}

// CLI options
interface CliOptions {
  command: "deploy" | "revert" | "help";
  directory: string;
  dryRun: boolean;
  agents: AgentId[] | null; // null means all detected
  verbose: boolean;
}
```

#### File 2: `src/config/agents.ts`

Static registry of all supported agents. Uses `{home}` as a placeholder that gets resolved at runtime.

```ts
const AGENT_REGISTRY: AgentConfig[] = [
  {
    id: "claude-code",
    displayName: "Claude Code",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.claude/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{home}\\.claude\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: { posix: "{home}/.claude", windows: "{home}\\.claude" },
    detectBinary: "claude",
  },
  {
    id: "codex",
    displayName: "OpenAI Codex",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.codex/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{home}\\.codex\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: { posix: "{home}/.codex", windows: "{home}\\.codex" },
    detectBinary: "codex",
  },
  {
    id: "gemini-cli",
    displayName: "Gemini CLI",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.gemini/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{home}\\.gemini\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: { posix: "{home}/.gemini", windows: "{home}\\.gemini" },
    detectBinary: "gemini",
  },
  {
    id: "antigravity",
    displayName: "Antigravity",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.gemini/antigravity/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{home}\\.gemini\\antigravity\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: {
      posix: "{home}/.gemini/antigravity",
      windows: "{home}\\.gemini\\antigravity",
    },
    detectBinary: null,
  },
  {
    id: "opencode",
    displayName: "OpenCode",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.config/opencode/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{appdata}\\opencode\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: {
      posix: "{home}/.config/opencode",
      windows: "{appdata}\\opencode",
    },
    detectBinary: "opencode",
  },
  {
    id: "github-copilot",
    displayName: "GitHub Copilot",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.copilot/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{home}\\.copilot\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: { posix: "{home}/.copilot", windows: "{home}\\.copilot" },
    detectBinary: "github-copilot",
  },
];
```

Note the `{appdata}` placeholder for OpenCode on Windows -- this maps to `%APPDATA%` via `process.env.APPDATA`.

#### File 3: `src/config/manifest.ts`

Manifest loading and validation. Reads `inception.json` from a given directory, validates its shape, and returns a typed `Manifest`.

Key logic:
- `fs.readFileSync(path.join(dir, "inception.json"), "utf-8")`
- JSON.parse with try/catch for syntax errors
- Validate that `skills` is an array, each entry has `name`, `path`, `agents`
- Validate that each `agents` entry is a known `AgentId`
- Return typed `Manifest` or throw with a clear error message

### Phase 2: Core Logic

#### File 4: `src/core/resolve.ts`

Cross-platform path resolution. This is the trickiest part.

Key functions:

**`resolveHome(): string`** -- The critical function for sudo support:
```
1. If platform is win32: return os.homedir()
2. If SUDO_USER is set:
   a. Read /etc/passwd or use os.userInfo() with SUDO_USER
   b. Construct home as /home/{SUDO_USER} (Linux) or /Users/{SUDO_USER} (macOS)
   c. Fallback: use HOME env var if SUDO_USER home doesn't exist
3. Otherwise: return os.homedir()
```

**`resolveAgentPath(agent: AgentConfig, skillName: string): string`** -- replaces `{home}`, `{appdata}`, `{name}` placeholders in the path template.

**`getPlatformKey(): "posix" | "windows"`** -- returns platform key based on `process.platform`.

**`getDeployMethod(): "symlink" | "copy"`** -- returns "copy" on win32, "symlink" on everything else.

#### File 5: `src/core/detect.ts`

Agent detection logic.

**`detectInstalledAgents(home: string): AgentId[]`**
1. Iterate over `AGENT_REGISTRY`
2. For each agent, resolve its `detectPaths` for the current platform
3. Check if that directory exists (`fs.existsSync`)
4. If not found by directory, check if `detectBinary` is in PATH using `child_process.execFileSync("which", [binary])` (POSIX) or `where.exe` (Windows), wrapped in try/catch
5. Return array of detected agent IDs

**`filterAgents(manifest: Manifest, detected: AgentId[]): SkillEntry[]`** -- for each skill in the manifest, intersect its `agents` array with detected agents.

#### File 6: `src/core/deploy.ts`

The deploy orchestrator.

**`planDeploy(manifest: Manifest, sourceDir: string, detectedAgents: AgentId[]): DeployAction[]`**
- For each skill entry in manifest
- For each agent in skill.agents that is also in detectedAgents
- Resolve the target path
- Create a `DeployAction` with source (`path.resolve(sourceDir, skill.path)`), target, and method

**`executeDeploy(actions: DeployAction[], dryRun: boolean): void`**
- For each action:
  1. If target exists (symlink, directory, or file): remove it (`fs.rmSync` with `recursive: true`)
  2. Ensure parent directory exists (`fs.mkdirSync` with `recursive: true`)
  3. If method is "symlink": `fs.symlinkSync(source, target, "dir")`
  4. If method is "copy": recursive copy using `fs.cpSync(source, target, { recursive: true })` (Node 20.13+)
  5. In dry-run mode: print what would happen instead of executing

**Edge cases handled:**
- Source path does not exist: throw with message identifying the missing skill directory
- Target parent directory does not exist: create it (the agent might not have a `skills/` dir yet even if the agent config dir exists)
- Symlink already exists pointing elsewhere: remove and re-create
- Partial deploy failure: continue deploying remaining actions, collect errors, report all at the end

#### File 7: `src/core/revert.ts`

**`planRevert(manifest: Manifest, detectedAgents: AgentId[]): RevertAction[]`**
- Same iteration as deploy, but only needs target paths

**`executeRevert(actions: RevertAction[], dryRun: boolean): void`**
- For each action:
  1. Check if target exists
  2. If it is a symlink: `fs.unlinkSync(target)`
  3. If it is a directory (Windows copy case): `fs.rmSync(target, { recursive: true })`
  4. If it does not exist: print a warning and skip
  5. In dry-run mode: print what would be removed

### Phase 3: CLI Entry Point

#### File 8: `src/index.ts`

The main entry point. Starts with a shebang line `#!/usr/bin/env node`.

**Argument parsing** (no framework, just `process.argv`):

```
Usage: inception-engine <directory> [options]
       inception-engine revert <directory> [options]

Options:
  --dry-run        Show what would be done without doing it
  --agents <list>  Comma-separated list of agent IDs to target
  --verbose        Show detailed output
  --help           Show this help message
```

Parsing logic:
```
const args = process.argv.slice(2)
1. If args is empty or contains --help: print usage, exit 0
2. If args[0] === "revert": command = "revert", directory = args[1]
3. Otherwise: command = "deploy", directory = args[0]
4. Walk remaining args for --dry-run, --agents, --verbose flags
5. Resolve directory to absolute path via path.resolve
6. Validate directory exists and contains inception.json
```

**Main execution flow:**
```
1. Parse CLI options
2. Load manifest from directory
3. Resolve home directory (sudo-aware)
4. Detect installed agents (or use --agents filter)
5. Plan actions (deploy or revert)
6. If no actions: print "No matching agents detected" and exit
7. Print summary of planned actions
8. Execute actions (respecting --dry-run)
9. Print completion summary
```

**Output formatting** -- simple console.log with prefixes:
- `[deploy]` green checkmark for successful deploys
- `[skip]` yellow for skipped items
- `[error]` red for failures
- `[dry-run]` cyan prefix in dry-run mode
- Use ANSI codes directly (no chalk dependency): `\x1b[32m` etc., with a `supportsColor()` check that falls back to plain text when stdout is not a TTY.

### Phase 4: Test Skills and Manifest

#### File 9: `skills/jedi-force-powers/SKILL.md`

```markdown
---
name: jedi-force-powers
description: Channel the Force to write elegant, powerful code
---

# Jedi Force Powers

You are a Jedi Master of code. Apply these Force-powered techniques:

## Force Refactor
When asked to refactor code, feel the code's energy flow. Identify disturbances
(code smells) and bring balance by applying the principle of least surprise.
Always leave the codebase more harmonious than you found it.

## Mind Trick
When explaining complex concepts, use the Jedi mind trick: start with what the
reader already knows, then gently guide them to the new understanding.
"This is not the bug you are looking for" -- unless it actually is.

## Lightsaber Precision
Cut through complexity with surgical precision. Every line of code should serve
a purpose. If a function is longer than a lightsaber, it needs splitting.

## The Force Test
Before committing, ask: "Would Yoda approve of this diff?" If the answer

## 4. Edge Cases and Error Handling

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| **`sudo npx inception-engine .`** | Read `SUDO_USER` env var, resolve their home via `/etc/passwd` pattern (`/home/$SUDO_USER` on Linux, `/Users/$SUDO_USER` on macOS). Print a notice: "Running as root, deploying to /Users/damian's home directory." |

| **Target skill dir already exists as a real directory (not symlink)** | Remove it with `fs.rmSync({ recursive: true })` before creating symlink. Print a warning that a non-symlinked directory was replaced. |
| **Agent skills parent dir missing** | Create it with `fs.mkdirSync({ recursive: true })`. For example, if `~/.claude/skills/` doesn't exist yet, create it. |
| **Source skill path missing** | Fail fast with error: `"Skill directory not found: skills/jedi-force-powers"`. Do not proceed with other skills for the same agent -- but do proceed with other agents. Actually, fail for that skill entirely and continue with remaining skills. |
| **Manifest missing** | Print: `"No inception.json found in <directory>. Are you pointing to the right repo?"` and exit 1. |
| **No agents detected** | Print: `"No supported AI agents detected on this system. Install one of: Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, ..."` and exit 0 (not an error). |
| **Partial failure** | Collect all errors, deploy everything possible, print summary with successes and failures at the end. Exit 1 if any failures occurred. |
| **Permission denied creating symlink** | Catch EPERM/EACCES, suggest running with sudo (POSIX) or as Administrator (Windows). |
| **Circular symlink** | Check with `fs.lstatSync` before removal. If target is a symlink, use `fs.unlinkSync` (not `fs.rmSync`). |

1. `package.json` and `tsconfig.json` -- project scaffolding

3. `src/config/agents.ts` -- agent registry (static data)

11. `skills/*/SKILL.md` -- three test skills
12. `README.md` -- updated with full documentation
13. `.gitignore` -- add `dist/`

Each file is small (50-150 lines) and has clear dependencies, making this straightforward to implement sequentially.

## 6. Key Technical Notes

**On `fs.cpSync`**: Available since Node 16.7.0, stable since 20.13.0. Since we target Node >= 20, this is safe. It handles recursive directory copying natively, avoiding the need for a third-party `fs-extra` dependency.

**On symlinks and `"dir"` type**: The third argument to `fs.symlinkSync` should be `"dir"` on Windows (it matters for NTFS junction behavior) and is ignored on POSIX. Always pass it for cross-platform correctness.

**On color output**: Use a minimal inline check: `process.stdout.isTTY && !process.env.NO_COLOR`. This respects the `NO_COLOR` convention (https://no-color.org/).

**On the shebang**: `#!/usr/bin/env node` at the top of `src/index.ts`. TypeScript will strip it during compilation. It must be present in the compiled `dist/index.js` -- tsc preserves shebangs when they appear on line 1.

Good -- Node 25.8.1 fully supports `--experimental-strip-types` (and in fact native TypeScript stripping is stable by Node 25). Now I have everything I need to produce the plan.

---

# Inception Engine -- Implementation Plan

## 1. Design Decisions (Resolved)

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Manifest file name | `inception.json` | Short, unambiguous, follows `package.json` / `tsconfig.json` convention |
| Symlink strategy | Symlink the entire skill directory (POSIX); recursive copy on Windows | One symlink per skill is simpler, atomic, and easier to revert. A directory symlink preserves future multi-file skills. |
| Agent detection | Config directory existence check first, binary-in-PATH as secondary | Config dirs are created on first run for all listed agents; binary check catches agents installed but never launched. |
| TypeScript execution | `node --experimental-strip-types` for development; compile to JS for the published npm package | Node 25 supports this natively. The `bin` entry in package.json points to the compiled `dist/index.js` so npx works on Node 20+ without flags. |
| Package bin entry | `"bin": { "inception-engine": "./dist/index.js" }` | Standard approach. A `prepublishOnly` script compiles TS to JS. |
| Home directory resolution under sudo | `SUDO_USER` env var on POSIX to get real user's home; `os.homedir()` as fallback | Running `sudo npx inception-engine .` would resolve `~` to `/root` without this. |

## 2. Project Setup

### 2a. `package.json`

```jsonc
{
  "name": "inception-engine",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Deploy AI agent skills from a git repo to user home directories",
  "license": "MIT",
  "author": "Damian Piatkowski",
  "type": "module",
  "bin": {
    "inception-engine": "./dist/index.js"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20.0.0"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc",
    "dev": "node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "npm run build"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "typescript": "^5.8.0"
  }
}
```

Key points:
- `"type": "module"` for ESM throughout.
- Zero runtime dependencies. Only `typescript` as a dev dependency.
- `"files": ["dist"]` keeps the published package lean.
- `engines.node >= 20` because that is the oldest Active LTS that supports the ESM features we need. The compiled JS output targets ES2024 which Node 20 handles.

### 2b. `tsconfig.json`

```jsonc
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2024",
    "module": "NodeNext",
    "moduleResolution": "NodeNext",
    "outDir": "./dist",
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "declaration": true,
    "strict": true,
    "erasableSyntaxOnly": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "verbatimModuleSyntax": true
  },
  "include": ["src"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}
```

`verbatimModuleSyntax` enforces `import type` for type-only imports, which pairs with `erasableSyntaxOnly` to guarantee all type syntax is purely erasable.

## 3. File-by-File Implementation Order

The implementation proceeds in dependency order -- types first, then config, then core logic, then CLI entry point, then test content.

### Phase 1: Types and Configuration

#### File 1: `src/types.ts`

Defines all shared types using only erasable syntax (interfaces, type aliases -- no enums).

```ts
// Agent identifiers as a union type (not an enum)
type AgentId =
  | "claude-code"
  | "codex"
  | "gemini-cli"
  | "antigravity"
  | "opencode"
  | "github-copilot";

// Manifest types
interface SkillEntry {
  name: string;
  path: string;
  agents: AgentId[];
}

interface Manifest {
  skills: SkillEntry[];
  mcpServers: unknown[];   // placeholder for future
  agentRules: unknown[];   // placeholder for future
}

// Agent registry types
interface AgentPaths {
  posix: string;   // template with {home} and {name} placeholders
  windows: string; // template with {home} and {name} placeholders
}

interface AgentConfig {
  id: AgentId;
  displayName: string;
  skills: AgentPaths;
  detectPaths: {
    posix: string;  // directory to check, e.g. "{home}/.claude"
    windows: string;
  };
  detectBinary: string | null; // binary name to look up in PATH
}

// Operation result types
interface DeployAction {
  skill: string;
  agent: AgentId;
  source: string;
  target: string;
  method: "symlink" | "copy";
}

interface RevertAction {
  skill: string;
  agent: AgentId;
  target: string;
}

// CLI options
interface CliOptions {
  command: "deploy" | "revert" | "help";
  directory: string;
  dryRun: boolean;
  agents: AgentId[] | null; // null means all detected
  verbose: boolean;
}
```

#### File 2: `src/config/agents.ts`

Static registry of all supported agents. Uses `{home}` as a placeholder that gets resolved at runtime.

```ts
const AGENT_REGISTRY: AgentConfig[] = [
  {
    id: "claude-code",
    displayName: "Claude Code",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.claude/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{home}\\.claude\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: { posix: "{home}/.claude", windows: "{home}\\.claude" },
    detectBinary: "claude",
  },
  {
    id: "codex",
    displayName: "OpenAI Codex",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.codex/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{home}\\.codex\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: { posix: "{home}/.codex", windows: "{home}\\.codex" },
    detectBinary: "codex",
  },
  {
    id: "gemini-cli",
    displayName: "Gemini CLI",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.gemini/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{home}\\.gemini\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: { posix: "{home}/.gemini", windows: "{home}\\.gemini" },
    detectBinary: "gemini",
  },
  {
    id: "antigravity",
    displayName: "Antigravity",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.gemini/antigravity/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{home}\\.gemini\\antigravity\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: {
      posix: "{home}/.gemini/antigravity",
      windows: "{home}\\.gemini\\antigravity",
    },
    detectBinary: null,
  },
  {
    id: "opencode",
    displayName: "OpenCode",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.config/opencode/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{appdata}\\opencode\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: {
      posix: "{home}/.config/opencode",
      windows: "{appdata}\\opencode",
    },
    detectBinary: "opencode",
  },
  {
    id: "github-copilot",
    displayName: "GitHub Copilot",
    skills: {
      posix: "{home}/.copilot/skills/{name}",
      windows: "{home}\\.copilot\\skills\\{name}",
    },
    detectPaths: { posix: "{home}/.copilot", windows: "{home}\\.copilot" },
    detectBinary: "github-copilot",
  },

```

Note the `{appdata}` placeholder for OpenCode on Windows -- this maps to `%APPDATA%` via `process.env.APPDATA`.

#### File 3: `src/config/manifest.ts`

Manifest loading and validation. Reads `inception.json` from a given directory, validates its shape, and returns a typed `Manifest`.

Key logic:
- `fs.readFileSync(path.join(dir, "inception.json"), "utf-8")`
- JSON.parse with try/catch for syntax errors
- Validate that `skills` is an array, each entry has `name`, `path`, `agents`
- Validate that each `agents` entry is a known `AgentId`
- Return typed `Manifest` or throw with a clear error message

### Phase 2: Core Logic

#### File 4: `src/core/resolve.ts`

Cross-platform path resolution. This is the trickiest part.

Key functions:

**`resolveHome(): string`** -- The critical function for sudo support:
```
1. If platform is win32: return os.homedir()
2. If SUDO_USER is set:
   a. Read /etc/passwd or use os.userInfo() with SUDO_USER
   b. Construct home as /home/{SUDO_USER} (Linux) or /Users/{SUDO_USER} (macOS)
   c. Fallback: use HOME env var if SUDO_USER home doesn't exist
3. Otherwise: return os.homedir()
```

**`resolveAgentPath(agent: AgentConfig, skillName: string): string`** -- replaces `{home}`, `{appdata}`, `{name}` placeholders in the path template.

**`getPlatformKey(): "posix" | "windows"`** -- returns platform key based on `process.platform`.

**`getDeployMethod(): "symlink" | "copy"`** -- returns "copy" on win32, "symlink" on everything else.

#### File 5: `src/core/detect.ts`

Agent detection logic.

**`detectInstalledAgents(home: string): AgentId[]`**
1. Iterate over `AGENT_REGISTRY`
2. For each agent, resolve its `detectPaths` for the current platform
3. Check if that directory exists (`fs.existsSync`)
4. If not found by directory, check if `detectBinary` is in PATH using `child_process.execFileSync("which", [binary])` (POSIX) or `where.exe` (Windows), wrapped in try/catch
5. Return array of detected agent IDs

**`filterAgents(manifest: Manifest, detected: AgentId[]): SkillEntry[]`** -- for each skill in the manifest, intersect its `agents` array with detected agents.

#### File 6: `src/core/deploy.ts`

The deploy orchestrator.

**`planDeploy(manifest: Manifest, sourceDir: string, detectedAgents: AgentId[]): DeployAction[]`**
- For each skill entry in manifest
- For each agent in skill.agents that is also in detectedAgents
- Resolve the target path
- Create a `DeployAction` with source (`path.resolve(sourceDir, skill.path)`), target, and method

**`executeDeploy(actions: DeployAction[], dryRun: boolean): void`**
- For each action:
  1. If target exists (symlink, directory, or file): remove it (`fs.rmSync` with `recursive: true`)
  2. Ensure parent directory exists (`fs.mkdirSync` with `recursive: true`)
  3. If method is "symlink": `fs.symlinkSync(source, target, "dir")`
  4. If method is "copy": recursive copy using `fs.cpSync(source, target, { recursive: true })` (Node 20.13+)
  5. In dry-run mode: print what would happen instead of executing

**Edge cases handled:**
- Source path does not exist: throw with message identifying the missing skill directory
- Target parent directory does not exist: create it (the agent might not have a `skills/` dir yet even if the agent config dir exists)
- Symlink already exists pointing elsewhere: remove and re-create
- Partial deploy failure: continue deploying remaining actions, collect errors, report all at the end

#### File 7: `src/core/revert.ts`

**`planRevert(manifest: Manifest, detectedAgents: AgentId[]): RevertAction[]`**
- Same iteration as deploy, but only needs target paths

**`executeRevert(actions: RevertAction[], dryRun: boolean): void`**
- For each action:
  1. Check if target exists
  2. If it is a symlink: `fs.unlinkSync(target)`
  3. If it is a directory (Windows copy case): `fs.rmSync(target, { recursive: true })`
  4. If it does not exist: print a warning and skip
  5. In dry-run mode: print what would be removed

### Phase 3: CLI Entry Point

#### File 8: `src/index.ts`

The main entry point. Starts with a shebang line `#!/usr/bin/env node`.

**Argument parsing** (no framework, just `process.argv`):

```
Usage: inception-engine <directory> [options]
       inception-engine revert <directory> [options]

Options:
  --dry-run        Show what would be done without doing it
  --agents <list>  Comma-separated list of agent IDs to target
  --verbose        Show detailed output
  --help           Show this help message
```

Parsing logic:
```
const args = process.argv.slice(2)
1. If args is empty or contains --help: print usage, exit 0
2. If args[0] === "revert": command = "revert", directory = args[1]
3. Otherwise: command = "deploy", directory = args[0]
4. Walk remaining args for --dry-run, --agents, --verbose flags
5. Resolve directory to absolute path via path.resolve
6. Validate directory exists and contains inception.json
```

**Main execution flow:**
```
1. Parse CLI options
2. Load manifest from directory
3. Resolve home directory (sudo-aware)
4. Detect installed agents (or use --agents filter)
5. Plan actions (deploy or revert)
6. If no actions: print "No matching agents detected" and exit
7. Print summary of planned actions
8. Execute actions (respecting --dry-run)
9. Print completion summary
```

**Output formatting** -- simple console.log with prefixes:
- `[deploy]` green checkmark for successful deploys
- `[skip]` yellow for skipped items
- `[error]` red for failures
- `[dry-run]` cyan prefix in dry-run mode
- Use ANSI codes directly (no chalk dependency): `\x1b[32m` etc., with a `supportsColor()` check that falls back to plain text when stdout is not a TTY.

### Phase 4: Test Skills and Manifest

#### File 9: `skills/jedi-force-powers/SKILL.md`

```markdown
---
name: jedi-force-powers
description: Channel the Force to write elegant, powerful code
---

# Jedi Force Powers

You are a Jedi Master of code. Apply these Force-powered techniques:

# Inception Engine - Implementation Plan

## Context

Build a cross-platform CLI tool (`npx inception-engine <directory>`) that deploys AI agent skills from a git repo to the correct user-level directories for each detected agent. The repo contains a manifest (`inception.json`) describing available skills; the tool symlinks (POSIX) or copies (Windows) them into each agent's skill directory. Only skills are implemented now; the manifest schema reserves space for MCP servers and agent rules.

## Project Setup

### `package.json`
- `"type": "module"`, zero runtime dependencies, `typescript` as devDependency
- `"bin": { "inception-engine": "./dist/index.js" }`
- Scripts: `build` (tsc), `dev` (node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts), `prepublishOnly` (build)
- `"engines": { "node": ">=22.0.0" }`

### `tsconfig.json`
- Target ES2024, module NodeNext, `erasableSyntaxOnly: true`, `verbatimModuleSyntax: true`
- `strict: true`, `outDir: ./dist`, `rootDir: ./src`

## File Structure

```
src/
  index.ts              # CLI entry point (shebang, argv parsing, main flow)
  types.ts              # All shared types (AgentId union, Manifest, AgentConfig, etc.)
  config/
    agents.ts           # Static agent registry with paths per platform
    manifest.ts         # Load and validate inception.json
  core/
    resolve.ts          # Cross-platform path resolution, sudo-aware home detection
    detect.ts           # Detect which agents are installed
    deploy.ts           # Plan and execute deploy (symlink/copy)
    revert.ts           # Plan and execute revert (remove deployed skills)
skills/                 # Example/test skills
  jedi-force-powers/SKILL.md
  time-traveler-debug/SKILL.md
  rubber-duck-deluxe/SKILL.md
inception.json          # Example manifest at repo root
```

## Agent Registry

| Agent | ID | User Skills Path (POSIX) | User Skills Path (Windows) | Detect Dir | Binary |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code | `claude-code` | `~/.claude/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\{name}` | `~/.claude` | `claude` |
| OpenAI Codex | `codex` | `~/.codex/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\skills\{name}` | `~/.codex` | `codex` |
| Gemini CLI | `gemini-cli` | `~/.gemini/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini` | `gemini` |
| Antigravity | `antigravity` | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\antigravity\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini/antigravity` | `null` |
| OpenCode | `opencode` | `~/.config/opencode/skills/{name}` | `%APPDATA%\opencode\skills\{name}` | `~/.config/opencode` | `opencode` |
| GitHub Copilot | `github-copilot` | `~/.copilot/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.copilot\skills\{name}` | `~/.copilot` | `github-copilot` |

## Manifest Format (`inception.json`)

```json
{
  "skills": [
    { "name": "jedi-force-powers", "path": "skills/jedi-force-powers", "agents": ["claude-code", "codex", ...] }
  ],
  "mcpServers": [],
  "agentRules": []
}
```

## Core Logic

### Path Resolution (`src/core/resolve.ts`)
- **Sudo handling**: Read `SUDO_USER` env var on POSIX, resolve home as `/Users/{user}` (macOS) or `/home/{user}` (Linux). Fallback to `os.homedir()`.
- **Windows**: `os.homedir()` works correctly even when elevated. `%APPDATA%` from `process.env.APPDATA` for OpenCode.
- **Platform key**: `process.platform === "win32"` ? `"windows"` : `"posix"`

### Agent Detection (`src/core/detect.ts`)
1. Check if agent's config directory exists
2. Fallback: check if agent binary is in PATH (`which`/`where.exe`)
3. Return list of detected `AgentId`s

### Deploy (`src/core/deploy.ts`)
1. Plan: for each skill x each matching detected agent, create a `DeployAction` (source, target, method)
2. Execute: for each action:
   - Remove existing target (symlink via `unlinkSync`, dir via `rmSync({recursive})`)
   - Create parent dir (`mkdirSync({recursive})`)
   - POSIX: `symlinkSync(source, target, "dir")`
   - Windows: `cpSync(source, target, {recursive})`
3. Dry-run: print actions without executing

### Revert (`src/core/revert.ts`)
1. Plan: same iteration, produce `RevertAction` (target paths)
2. Execute: remove symlinks/directories at target paths
3. Skip with warning if target doesn't exist

## CLI Interface

```
Usage: inception-engine <directory> [options]
       inception-engine revert <directory> [options]

Options:
  --dry-run        Show what would be done
  --agents <list>  Comma-separated agent IDs to target
  --verbose        Detailed output
  --help           Show help
```

No CLI framework. Simple `process.argv` parsing. Minimal ANSI color (respecting `NO_COLOR` and TTY check).

## Test Skills

Three fun example skills (all targeting all 6 agents):
- **jedi-force-powers** - Force-powered coding techniques
- **time-traveler-debug** - Temporal debugging via git archaeology
- **rubber-duck-deluxe** - Premium rubber duck debugging with attitude

## Edge Cases

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| Running with sudo | Resolve real user's home via `SUDO_USER` |
| Target dir already exists (not symlink) | Remove and replace, print warning |
| Agent skills parent dir missing | Create with `mkdirSync({recursive})` |
| Source skill path missing | Error for that skill, continue with others |
| No manifest found | Print helpful error, exit 1 |
| No agents detected | Print message listing agents to install, exit 0 |
| Permission denied | Suggest sudo (POSIX) or Administrator (Windows) |

## README.md Structure
1. Title + description
2. Quick start (`npx inception-engine .`)
3. Agent compatibility matrix table
4. Manifest format docs
5. Creating skills (SKILL.md format)
6. CLI reference
7. Cross-platform behavior (symlink vs copy)
8. Sudo / privilege escalation notes
9. License

## Implementation Order
1. `package.json` + `tsconfig.json`
2. `src/types.ts`
3. `src/config/agents.ts`
4. `src/config/manifest.ts`
5. `src/core/resolve.ts`
6. `src/core/detect.ts`
7. `src/core/deploy.ts`
8. `src/core/revert.ts`
9. `src/index.ts`
10. `inception.json`
11. `skills/*/SKILL.md` (3 skills)
12. `README.md`
13. Update `.gitignore` (add `dist/`)

## Verification
1. `npm install` then `npm run build` - compiles without errors
2. `node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts --help` - prints usage
3. `node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts . --dry-run` - shows planned actions for detected agents
4. `node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts .` - deploys skills, verify symlinks exist
5. `node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts revert . --dry-run` - shows what would be removed
6. `node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts revert .` - removes deployed skills

Good question. Yes, we absolutely can publish a TypeScript project to npm - but the `bin` entry must point to compiled JavaScript. Here's how it works:

- **Source**: TypeScript in `src/`
- **Published package**: compiled JS in `dist/` (via `tsc` in `prepublishOnly` script)
- **`bin` in package.json** points to `dist/index.js` - plain JS, runs on any Node.js
- **`"files": ["dist"]`** ensures only compiled output is published to npm

So when someone runs `npx inception-engine .`, npm downloads the package and executes the compiled JS. No TypeScript runtime needed for consumers. The erasable-syntax TypeScript + `node --experimental-strip-types` is only for **development** convenience (running `src/index.ts` directly without a build step).

    deploy.ts           # Plan and execute deploy (symlink/copy)
    revert.ts           # Plan and execute revert (remove deployed skills)
skills/                 # Example/test skills
  jedi-force-powers/SKILL.md
  time-traveler-debug/SKILL.md
  rubber-duck-deluxe/SKILL.md
inception.json          # Example manifest at repo root
```

## Agent Registry

| Agent | ID | User Skills Path (POSIX) | User Skills Path (Windows) | Detect Dir | Binary |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code | `claude-code` | `~/.claude/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\{name}` | `~/.claude` | `claude` |
| OpenAI Codex | `codex` | `~/.codex/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\skills\{name}` | `~/.codex` | `codex` |
| Gemini CLI | `gemini-cli` | `~/.gemini/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini` | `gemini` |
| Antigravity | `antigravity` | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\antigravity\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini/antigravity` | `null` |
| OpenCode | `opencode` | `~/.config/opencode/skills/{name}` | `%APPDATA%\opencode\skills\{name}` | `~/.config/opencode` | `opencode` |
| GitHub Copilot | `github-copilot` | `~/.copilot/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.copilot\skills\{name}` | `~/.copilot` | `github-copilot` |

## Manifest Format (`inception.json`)

```json
{
  "skills": [
    { "name": "jedi-force-powers", "path": "skills/jedi-force-powers", "agents": ["claude-code", "codex", ...] }
  ],
  "mcpServers": [],
  "agentRules": []
}
```

## Core Logic

### Path Resolution (`src/core/resolve.ts`)
- **Sudo handling**: Read `SUDO_USER` env var on POSIX, resolve home as `/Users/{user}` (macOS) or `/home/{user}` (Linux). Fallback to `os.homedir()`.
- **Windows**: `os.homedir()` works correctly even when elevated. `%APPDATA%` from `process.env.APPDATA` for OpenCode.
- **Platform key**: `process.platform === "win32"` ? `"windows"` : `"posix"`

### Agent Detection (`src/core/detect.ts`)
1. Check if agent's config directory exists
2. Fallback: check if agent binary is in PATH (`which`/`where.exe`)
3. Return list of detected `AgentId`s

### Deploy (`src/core/deploy.ts`)
1. Plan: for each skill x each matching detected agent, create a `DeployAction` (source, target, method)
2. Execute: for each action:
   - Remove existing target (symlink via `unlinkSync`, dir via `rmSync({recursive})`)
   - Create parent dir (`mkdirSync({recursive})`)
   - POSIX: `symlinkSync(source, target, "dir")`
   - Windows: `cpSync(source, target, {recursive})`
3. Dry-run: print actions without executing

### Revert (`src/core/revert.ts`)
1. Plan: same iteration, produce `RevertAction` (target paths)
2. Execute: remove symlinks/directories at target paths
3. Skip with warning if target doesn't exist

## CLI Interface

```
Usage: inception-engine <directory> [options]
       inception-engine revert <directory> [options]

Options:
  --dry-run        Show what would be done
  --agents <list>  Comma-separated agent IDs to target
  --verbose        Detailed output
  --help           Show help
```

No CLI framework. Simple `process.argv` parsing. Minimal ANSI color (respecting `NO_COLOR` and TTY check).

## Test Skills

Three fun example skills (all targeting all 6 agents):
- **jedi-force-powers** - Force-powered coding techniques
- **time-traveler-debug** - Temporal debugging via git archaeology
- **rubber-duck-deluxe** - Premium rubber duck debugging with attitude

## Edge Cases

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| Running with sudo | Resolve real user's home via `SUDO_USER` |
| Target dir already exists (not symlink) | Remove and replace, print warning |
| Agent skills parent dir missing | Create with `mkdirSync({recursive})` |
| Source skill path missing | Error for that skill, continue with others |
| No manifest found | Print helpful error, exit 1 |
| No agents detected | Print message listing agents to install, exit 0 |
| Permission denied | Suggest sudo (POSIX) or Administrator (Windows) |

    detect.ts           # Detect which agents are installed
    deploy.ts           # Plan and execute deploy (symlink/copy)
    revert.ts           # Plan and execute revert (remove deployed skills)
skills/                 # Example/test skills
  jedi-force-powers/SKILL.md
  time-traveler-debug/SKILL.md
  rubber-duck-deluxe/SKILL.md
inception.json          # Example manifest at repo root
```

| Agent | ID | User Skills Path (POSIX) | User Skills Path (Windows) | Detect Dir | Binary |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code | `claude-code` | `~/.claude/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\{name}` | `~/.claude` | `claude` |
| OpenAI Codex | `codex` | `~/.codex/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\skills\{name}` | `~/.codex` | `codex` |
| Gemini CLI | `gemini-cli` | `~/.gemini/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini` | `gemini` |
| Antigravity | `antigravity` | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\antigravity\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini/antigravity` | `null` |
| OpenCode | `opencode` | `~/.config/opencode/skills/{name}` | `%APPDATA%\opencode\skills\{name}` | `~/.config/opencode` | `opencode` |
| GitHub Copilot | `github-copilot` | `~/.copilot/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.copilot\skills\{name}` | `~/.copilot` | `github-copilot` |

```json

  "skills": [
    { "name": "jedi-force-powers", "path": "skills/jedi-force-powers", "agents": ["claude-code", "codex", ...] }
  ],
  "mcpServers": [],
  "agentRules": []
}
```

## Core Logic

### Path Resolution (`src/core/resolve.ts`)
- **Sudo handling**: Read `SUDO_USER` env var on POSIX, resolve home as `/Users/{user}` (macOS) or `/home/{user}` (Linux). Fallback to `os.homedir()`.
- **Windows**: `os.homedir()` works correctly even when elevated. `%APPDATA%` from `process.env.APPDATA` for OpenCode.
- **Platform key**: `process.platform === "win32"` ? `"windows"` : `"posix"`

### Agent Detection (`src/core/detect.ts`)
1. Check if agent's config directory exists
2. Fallback: check if agent binary is in PATH (`which`/`where.exe`)
3. Return list of detected `AgentId`s

### Deploy (`src/core/deploy.ts`)
1. Plan: for each skill x each matching detected agent, create a `DeployAction` (source, target, method)
2. Execute: for each action:
   - Remove existing target (symlink via `unlinkSync`, dir via `rmSync({recursive})`)
   - Create parent dir (`mkdirSync({recursive})`)
   - POSIX: `symlinkSync(source, target, "dir")`
   - Windows: `cpSync(source, target, {recursive})`
3. Dry-run: print actions without executing

### Revert (`src/core/revert.ts`)
1. Plan: same iteration, produce `RevertAction` (target paths)
2. Execute: remove symlinks/directories at target paths
3. Skip with warning if target doesn't exist

## CLI Interface

```
Usage: inception-engine <directory> [options]
       inception-engine revert <directory> [options]

Options:
  --dry-run        Show what would be done
  --agents <list>  Comma-separated agent IDs to target
  --verbose        Detailed output
  --help           Show help
```

Three fun example skills (all targeting all 6 agents):
- **jedi-force-powers** - Force-powered coding techniques
- **time-traveler-debug** - Temporal debugging via git archaeology
- **rubber-duck-deluxe** - Premium rubber duck debugging with attitude

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| Running with sudo | Resolve real user's home via `SUDO_USER` |
| Target dir already exists (not symlink) | Remove and replace, print warning |
| Agent skills parent dir missing | Create with `mkdirSync({recursive})` |
| Source skill path missing | Error for that skill, continue with others |
| No manifest found | Print helpful error, exit 1 |
| No agents detected | Print message listing agents to install, exit 0 |
| Permission denied | Suggest sudo (POSIX) or Administrator (Windows) |

## README.md Structure
1. Title + description
2. Quick start (`npx inception-engine .`)
3. Agent compatibility matrix table
4. Manifest format docs
5. Creating skills (SKILL.md format)
6. CLI reference
7. Cross-platform behavior (symlink vs copy)
8. Sudo / privilege escalation notes
9. License

## Implementation Order
1. `package.json` + `tsconfig.json`
2. `src/types.ts`
3. `src/config/agents.ts`
4. `src/config/manifest.ts`
5. `src/core/resolve.ts`
6. `src/core/detect.ts`
7. `src/core/deploy.ts`

# Inception Engine - Implementation Plan

## Context

Build a cross-platform CLI tool (`npx inception-engine <directory>`) that deploys AI agent skills from a git repo to the correct user-level directories for each detected agent. The repo contains a manifest (`inception.json`) describing available skills; the tool symlinks (POSIX) or copies (Windows) them into each agent's skill directory. Only skills are implemented now; the manifest schema reserves space for MCP servers and agent rules.

## Project Setup

### `package.json`
- `"type": "module"`, zero runtime dependencies, `typescript` as devDependency
- `"bin": { "inception-engine": "./dist/index.js" }`
- Scripts: `build` (tsc), `dev` (node src/index.ts), `test` (node --test), `prepublishOnly` (build)
- `"engines": { "node": ">=23.6.0" }` - stable unflagged TypeScript stripping
- Dev: run `node src/index.ts` directly (no flags needed on Node 23.6+)
- Published: consumers run compiled JS from `dist/`, works on any modern Node

### `tsconfig.json`
- Target ES2024, module NodeNext, `erasableSyntaxOnly: true`, `verbatimModuleSyntax: true`
- `strict: true`, `outDir: ./dist`, `rootDir: ./src`

## File Structure

```
src/
  index.ts              # CLI entry point (shebang, argv parsing, main flow)
  types.ts              # All shared types (AgentId union, Manifest, AgentConfig, etc.)
  config/
    agents.ts           # Static agent registry with paths per platform
    manifest.ts         # Load and validate inception.json
  core/
    resolve.ts          # Cross-platform path resolution, sudo-aware home detection
    detect.ts           # Detect which agents are installed
    deploy.ts           # Plan and execute deploy (symlink/copy)
    revert.ts           # Plan and execute revert (remove deployed skills)
test/
  manifest.test.ts      # Manifest loading/validation tests
  resolve.test.ts       # Path resolution tests (mocked platform/env)
  detect.test.ts        # Agent detection tests
  deploy.test.ts        # Deploy planning + execution tests (using temp dirs)
  revert.test.ts        # Revert tests
limbo/                  # Sample skills directory (Inception: the deepest dream level)
  skills/
    jedi-force-powers/SKILL.md
    time-traveler-debug/SKILL.md
    rubber-duck-deluxe/SKILL.md
  inception.json        # Sample manifest
```

## Agent Registry

| Agent | ID | User Skills Path (POSIX) | User Skills Path (Windows) | Detect Dir | Binary |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code | `claude-code` | `~/.claude/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\{name}` | `~/.claude` | `claude` |
| OpenAI Codex | `codex` | `~/.codex/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\skills\{name}` | `~/.codex` | `codex` |
| Gemini CLI | `gemini-cli` | `~/.gemini/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini` | `gemini` |
| Antigravity | `antigravity` | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\antigravity\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini/antigravity` | `null` |
| OpenCode | `opencode` | `~/.config/opencode/skills/{name}` | `%APPDATA%\opencode\skills\{name}` | `~/.config/opencode` | `opencode` |
| GitHub Copilot | `github-copilot` | `~/.copilot/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.copilot\skills\{name}` | `~/.copilot` | `github-copilot` |

## Manifest Format (`inception.json`)

```json
{
  "skills": [
    { "name": "jedi-force-powers", "path": "skills/jedi-force-powers", "agents": ["claude-code", "codex", ...] }
  ],
  "mcpServers": [],
  "agentRules": []
}
```

## Core Logic

### Path Resolution (`src/core/resolve.ts`)
- **Default**: `os.homedir()` - works without any privilege escalation
- **Sudo graceful handling**: When `SUDO_USER` is set on POSIX, resolve to the real user's home (`/Users/{user}` on macOS, `/home/{user}` on Linux) so skills land in the right place even if someone runs with sudo

- **Platform key**: `process.platform === "win32"` ? `"windows"` : `"posix"`

1. Check if agent's config directory exists
2. Fallback: check if agent binary is in PATH (`which`/`where.exe`)
3. Return list of detected `AgentId`s

### Deploy (`src/core/deploy.ts`)
1. Plan: for each skill x each matching detected agent, create a `DeployAction` (source, target, method)
2. Execute: for each action:
   - Remove existing target (symlink via `unlinkSync`, dir via `rmSync({recursive})`)
   - Create parent dir (`mkdirSync({recursive})`)
   - POSIX: `symlinkSync(source, target, "dir")`
   - Windows: `cpSync(source, target, {recursive})`
3. Dry-run: print actions without executing

### Revert (`src/core/revert.ts`)
1. Plan: same iteration, produce `RevertAction` (target paths)
2. Execute: remove symlinks/directories at target paths
3. Skip with warning if target doesn't exist

## CLI Interface

```
Usage: inception-engine <directory> [options]
       inception-engine revert <directory> [options]

Options:
  --dry-run        Show what would be done
  --agents <list>  Comma-separated agent IDs to target
  --verbose        Detailed output
  --help           Show help
```

No CLI framework. Simple `process.argv` parsing. Minimal ANSI color (respecting `NO_COLOR` and TTY check).

## Test Skills (in `limbo/`)

Three fun example skills (all targeting all 6 agents):
- **jedi-force-powers** - Force-powered coding techniques
- **time-traveler-debug** - Temporal debugging via git archaeology
- **rubber-duck-deluxe** - Premium rubber duck debugging with attitude

## Tests (`node:test`)

Using Node's built-in test runner. Tests use temp directories for filesystem operations.

- **`test/manifest.test.ts`** - Valid manifest parsing, missing fields, unknown agent IDs, missing file
- **`test/resolve.test.ts`** - Home resolution with/without SUDO_USER, platform path templates, {appdata} substitution
- **`test/detect.test.ts`** - Detection via directory existence, binary lookup fallback, filtering by manifest agents
- **`test/deploy.test.ts`** - Plan generation, symlink creation in temp dir, overwrite existing, dry-run no-op, partial failure handling
- **`test/revert.test.ts`** - Remove symlinks, remove copied dirs, skip missing targets with warning

## Edge Cases

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| Running with sudo | Resolve real user's home via `SUDO_USER` (graceful, not required) |
| Running without sudo | `os.homedir()` - the default, normal path |
| Target dir already exists (not symlink) | Remove and replace, print warning |
| Agent skills parent dir missing | Create with `mkdirSync({recursive})` |
| Source skill path missing | Error for that skill, continue with others |
| No manifest found | Print helpful error, exit 1 |
| No agents detected | Print message listing agents to install, exit 0 |
| Permission denied | Suggest sudo (POSIX) or Administrator (Windows) |

## README.md Structure
1. Title + description
2. Quick start (`npx inception-engine .`)
3. Agent compatibility matrix table
4. Manifest format docs
5. Creating skills (SKILL.md format)
6. CLI reference
7. Cross-platform behavior (symlink vs copy)
8. Sudo / privilege escalation notes
9. License

## Implementation Order
1. `package.json` + `tsconfig.json`
2. `src/types.ts`
3. `src/config/agents.ts`
4. `src/config/manifest.ts`
5. `src/core/resolve.ts`
6. `src/core/detect.ts`
7. `src/core/deploy.ts`
8. `src/core/revert.ts`
9. `src/index.ts`
10. `limbo/inception.json` + `limbo/skills/*/SKILL.md` (3 skills)
11. `test/*.test.ts` (5 test files)
12. `README.md`
13. Update `.gitignore` (add `dist/`)

## Verification
1. `npm install` then `npm run build` - compiles without errors
2. `npm test` - all tests pass
3. `node src/index.ts --help` - prints usage
4. `node src/index.ts limbo --dry-run` - shows planned actions for detected agents
5. `node src/index.ts limbo` - deploys skills, verify symlinks exist
6. `node src/index.ts revert limbo --dry-run` - shows what would be removed
7. `node src/index.ts revert limbo` - removes deployed skills

Only skills are implemented now; the manifest schema reserves space for MCP servers and agent rules.

## Project Setup

### `package.json`
- `"type": "module"`, zero runtime dependencies, `typescript` as devDependency
- `"bin": { "inception-engine": "./dist/index.js" }`
- Scripts: `build` (tsc), `dev` (node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts), `prepublishOnly` (build)
- `"engines": { "node": ">=22.0.0" }` (consumers only need Node 22+ since they run compiled JS)
- Dev convenience: `node --experimental-strip-types` to run .ts directly during development

### `tsconfig.json`
- Target ES2024, module NodeNext, `erasableSyntaxOnly: true`, `verbatimModuleSyntax: true`
- `strict: true`, `outDir: ./dist`, `rootDir: ./src`

## File Structure

```
src/
  index.ts              # CLI entry point (shebang, argv parsing, main flow)
  types.ts              # All shared types (AgentId union, Manifest, AgentConfig, etc.)
  config/
    agents.ts           # Static agent registry with paths per platform
    manifest.ts         # Load and validate inception.json
  core/
    resolve.ts          # Cross-platform path resolution, sudo-aware home detection
    detect.ts           # Detect which agents are installed
    deploy.ts           # Plan and execute deploy (symlink/copy)
    revert.ts           # Plan and execute revert (remove deployed skills)
skills/                 # Example/test skills
  jedi-force-powers/SKILL.md
  time-traveler-debug/SKILL.md
  rubber-duck-deluxe/SKILL.md
inception.json          # Example manifest at repo root
```

## Agent Registry

| Agent | ID | User Skills Path (POSIX) | User Skills Path (Windows) | Detect Dir | Binary |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code | `claude-code` | `~/.claude/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\{name}` | `~/.claude` | `claude` |
| OpenAI Codex | `codex` | `~/.codex/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\skills\{name}` | `~/.codex` | `codex` |
| Gemini CLI | `gemini-cli` | `~/.gemini/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini` | `gemini` |
| Antigravity | `antigravity` | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\antigravity\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini/antigravity` | `null` |
| OpenCode | `opencode` | `~/.config/opencode/skills/{name}` | `%APPDATA%\opencode\skills\{name}` | `~/.config/opencode` | `opencode` |
| GitHub Copilot | `github-copilot` | `~/.copilot/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.copilot\skills\{name}` | `~/.copilot` | `github-copilot` |

## Manifest Format (`inception.json`)

```json
{
  "skills": [
    { "name": "jedi-force-powers", "path": "skills/jedi-force-powers", "agents": ["claude-code", "codex", ...] }
  ],
  "mcpServers": [],
  "agentRules": []
}
```

## Core Logic

### Path Resolution (`src/core/resolve.ts`)
- **Sudo handling**: Read `SUDO_USER` env var on POSIX, resolve home as `/Users/{user}` (macOS) or `/home/{user}` (Linux). Fallback to `os.homedir()`.
- **Windows**: `os.homedir()` works correctly even when elevated. `%APPDATA%` from `process.env.APPDATA` for OpenCode.
- **Platform key**: `process.platform === "win32"` ? `"windows"` : `"posix"`

### Agent Detection (`src/core/detect.ts`)
1. Check if agent's config directory exists
2. Fallback: check if agent binary is in PATH (`which`/`where.exe`)
3. Return list of detected `AgentId`s

### Deploy (`src/core/deploy.ts`)
1. Plan: for each skill x each matching detected agent, create a `DeployAction` (source, target, method)
2. Execute: for each action:
   - Remove existing target (symlink via `unlinkSync`, dir via `rmSync({recursive})`)
   - Create parent dir (`mkdirSync({recursive})`)
   - POSIX: `symlinkSync(source, target, "dir")`
   - Windows: `cpSync(source, target, {recursive})`
3. Dry-run: print actions without executing

### Revert (`src/core/revert.ts`)
1. Plan: same iteration, produce `RevertAction` (target paths)
2. Execute: remove symlinks/directories at target paths
3. Skip with warning if target doesn't exist

## CLI Interface

```
Usage: inception-engine <directory> [options]
       inception-engine revert <directory> [options]

Options:
  --dry-run        Show what would be done
  --agents <list>  Comma-separated agent IDs to target
  --verbose        Detailed output
  --help           Show help
```

No CLI framework. Simple `process.argv` parsing. Minimal ANSI color (respecting `NO_COLOR` and TTY check).

## Test Skills

Three fun example skills (all targeting all 6 agents):
- **jedi-force-powers** - Force-powered coding techniques
- **time-traveler-debug** - Temporal debugging via git archaeology
- **rubber-duck-deluxe** - Premium rubber duck debugging with attitude

## Edge Cases

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| Running with sudo | Resolve real user's home via `SUDO_USER` |
| Target dir already exists (not symlink) | Remove and replace, print warning |
| Agent skills parent dir missing | Create with `mkdirSync({recursive})` |
| Source skill path missing | Error for that skill, continue with others |
| No manifest found | Print helpful error, exit 1 |
| No agents detected | Print message listing agents to install, exit 0 |
| Permission denied | Suggest sudo (POSIX) or Administrator (Windows) |

## README.md Structure
1. Title + description
2. Quick start (`npx inception-engine .`)
3. Agent compatibility matrix table
4. Manifest format docs
5. Creating skills (SKILL.md format)
6. CLI reference
7. Cross-platform behavior (symlink vs copy)
8. Sudo / privilege escalation notes
9. License

## Implementation Order
1. `package.json` + `tsconfig.json`
2. `src/types.ts`
3. `src/config/agents.ts`
4. `src/config/manifest.ts`
5. `src/core/resolve.ts`
6. `src/core/detect.ts`
7. `src/core/deploy.ts`
8. `src/core/revert.ts`
9. `src/index.ts`
10. `inception.json`
11. `skills/*/SKILL.md` (3 skills)
12. `README.md`
13. Update `.gitignore` (add `dist/`)

## Verification
1. `npm install` then `npm run build` - compiles without errors
2. `node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts --help` - prints usage
3. `node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts . --dry-run` - shows planned actions for detected agents
4. `node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts .` - deploys skills, verify symlinks exist
5. `node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts revert . --dry-run` - shows what would be removed
6. `node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts revert .` - removes deployed skills

  deploy.test.ts        # Deploy planning + execution tests (using temp dirs)
  revert.test.ts        # Revert tests
limbo/                  # Sample skills directory (Inception: the deepest dream level)
  skills/
    jedi-force-powers/SKILL.md
    time-traveler-debug/SKILL.md
    rubber-duck-deluxe/SKILL.md
  inception.json        # Sample manifest
```

## Agent Registry

| Agent | ID | User Skills Path (POSIX) | User Skills Path (Windows) | Detect Dir | Binary |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code | `claude-code` | `~/.claude/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\{name}` | `~/.claude` | `claude` |
| OpenAI Codex | `codex` | `~/.codex/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\skills\{name}` | `~/.codex` | `codex` |
| Gemini CLI | `gemini-cli` | `~/.gemini/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini` | `gemini` |
| Antigravity | `antigravity` | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.gemini\antigravity\skills\{name}` | `~/.gemini/antigravity` | `null` |
| OpenCode | `opencode` | `~/.config/opencode/skills/{name}` | `%APPDATA%\opencode\skills\{name}` | `~/.config/opencode` | `opencode` |
| GitHub Copilot | `github-copilot` | `~/.copilot/skills/{name}` | `%USERPROFILE%\.copilot\skills\{name}` | `~/.copilot` | `github-copilot` |

## Manifest Format (`inception.json`)

```json
{
  "skills": [
    { "name": "jedi-force-powers", "path": "skills/jedi-force-powers", "agents": ["claude-code", "codex", ...] }
  ],
  "mcpServers": [],
  "agentRules": []
}
```

## Core Logic

### Path Resolution (`src/core/resolve.ts`)
- **Default**: `os.homedir()` - works without any privilege escalation
- **Sudo graceful handling**: When `SUDO_USER` is set on POSIX, resolve to the real user's home (`/Users/{user}` on macOS, `/home/{user}` on Linux) so skills land in the right place even if someone runs with sudo
- **Windows**: `os.homedir()` works correctly even when elevated. `%APPDATA%` from `process.env.APPDATA` for OpenCode.
- **Platform key**: `process.platform === "win32"` ? `"windows"` : `"posix"`

### Agent Detection (`src/core/detect.ts`)
1. Check if agent's config directory exists
2. Fallback: check if agent binary is in PATH (`which`/`where.exe`)
3. Return list of detected `AgentId`s

### Deploy (`src/core/deploy.ts`)
1. Plan: for each skill x each matching detected agent, create a `DeployAction` (source, target, method)
2. Execute: for each action:
   - Remove existing target (symlink via `unlinkSync`, dir via `rmSync({recursive})`)
   - Create parent dir (`mkdirSync({recursive})`)
   - POSIX: `symlinkSync(source, target, "dir")`
   - Windows: `cpSync(source, target, {recursive})`
3. Dry-run: print actions without executing

### Revert (`src/core/revert.ts`)
1. Plan: same iteration, produce `RevertAction` (target paths)
2. Execute: remove symlinks/directories at target paths
3. Skip with warning if target doesn't exist

## CLI Interface

```
Usage: inception-engine <directory> [options]
       inception-engine revert <directory> [options]

Options:
  --dry-run        Show what would be done
  --agents <list>  Comma-separated agent IDs to target
  --verbose        Detailed output
  --help           Show help
```

No CLI framework. Simple `process.argv` parsing. Minimal ANSI color (respecting `NO_COLOR` and TTY check).

## Test Skills (in `limbo/`)

Three fun example skills (all targeting all 6 agents):
- **jedi-force-powers** - Force-powered coding techniques
- **time-traveler-debug** - Temporal debugging via git archaeology
- **rubber-duck-deluxe** - Premium rubber duck debugging with attitude

## Tests (`node:test`)

Using Node's built-in test runner. Tests use temp directories for filesystem operations.

- **`test/manifest.test.ts`** - Valid manifest parsing, missing fields, unknown agent IDs, missing file
- **`test/resolve.test.ts`** - Home resolution with/without SUDO_USER, platform path templates, {appdata} substitution
- **`test/detect.test.ts`** - Detection via directory existence, binary lookup fallback, filtering by manifest agents
- **`test/deploy.test.ts`** - Plan generation, symlink creation in temp dir, overwrite existing, dry-run no-op, partial failure handling
- **`test/revert.test.ts`** - Remove symlinks, remove copied dirs, skip missing targets with warning

## Edge Cases

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| Running with sudo | Resolve real user's home via `SUDO_USER` (graceful, not required) |
| Running without sudo | `os.homedir()` - the default, normal path |
| Target dir already exists (not symlink) | Remove and replace, print warning |
| Agent skills parent dir missing | Create with `mkdirSync({recursive})` |
| Source skill path missing | Error for that skill, continue with others |
| No manifest found | Print helpful error, exit 1 |
| No agents detected | Print message listing agents to install, exit 0 |
| Permission denied | Suggest sudo (POSIX) or Administrator (Windows) |

## README.md Structure
1. Title + description
2. Quick start (`npx inception-engine .`)
3. Agent compatibility matrix table
4. Manifest format docs
5. Creating skills (SKILL.md format)
6. CLI reference
7. Cross-platform behavior (symlink vs copy)
8. Sudo / privilege escalation notes
9. License

## Implementation Order
1. `package.json` + `tsconfig.json`
2. `src/types.ts`
3. `src/config/agents.ts`
4. `src/config/manifest.ts`
5. `src/core/resolve.ts`

8. `src/core/revert.ts`
9. `src/index.ts`
10. `limbo/inception.json` + `limbo/skills/*/SKILL.md` (3 skills)
11. `test/*.test.ts` (5 test files)
12. `README.md`
13. Update `.gitignore` (add `dist/`)

## Verification
1. `npm install` then `npm run build` - compiles without errors
2. `npm test` - all tests pass
3. `node src/index.ts --help` - prints usage
4. `node src/index.ts limbo --dry-run` - shows planned actions for detected agents
5. `node src/index.ts limbo` - deploys skills, verify symlinks exist
6. `node src/index.ts revert limbo --dry-run` - shows what would be removed
7. `node src/index.ts revert limbo` - removes deployed skills

# Jedi Force Powers

You are a Jedi Master of code. Apply these Force-powered techniques:

## Force Refactor

When asked to refactor code, feel the code's energy flow. Identify disturbances
(code smells) and bring balance by applying the principle of least surprise.
